Title: Insights into angiosperm evolution and lineage-specialized lignan biosynthesis from the early-diverging Schisandra genome

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative dimerization step of dibenzylbutane lignan formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively dimerizes isoeugenol to form verrucosin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the lignan precursor verrucosin from isoeugenol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchLAC14 was prioritized by correlation with pregomisin accumulation in stems. 2. Heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris and in vitro assay showed SchLAC14 catalyzed oxidative dimerization of isoeugenol to verrucosin. 3. This supports its role in the first committed dimerization step toward DBCOD lignans.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS product identification against standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlated with high pregomisin accumulation in stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for dibenzylbutane precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four SchLAC paralogs with the same demonstrated activity; paper summary later refers to one SchLAC as representative of the core pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative dimerization step of dibenzylbutane lignan formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively dimerizes isoeugenol to form verrucosin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the lignan precursor verrucosin from isoeugenol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchLAC15 showed strong correlation with pregomisin-associated tissues. 2. Recombinant enzyme produced verrucosin from isoeugenol in vitro. 3. It is a candidate early-step enzyme for DBCOD lignan precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS product identification against standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlated with high pregomisin accumulation in stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for dibenzylbutane precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with SchLAC14/16/17 in assay.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative dimerization step of dibenzylbutane lignan formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively dimerizes isoeugenol to form verrucosin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the lignan precursor verrucosin from isoeugenol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchLAC16 was among the LACs highly correlated with pregomisin accumulation. 2. Biochemical assay demonstrated oxidative dimerization of isoeugenol to verrucosin. 3. This places SchLAC16 in the entry step to dibenzylbutane/DBCOD lignan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS product identification against standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlated with high pregomisin accumulation in stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for dibenzylbutane precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with SchLAC14/15/17 in assay.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative dimerization step of dibenzylbutane lignan formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively dimerizes isoeugenol to form verrucosin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the lignan precursor verrucosin from isoeugenol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchLAC17 had one of the strongest correlations with pregomisin accumulation. 2. In vitro assay confirmed conversion of isoeugenol to verrucosin. 3. SchLAC17 is part of the functionally characterized early DBCOD lignan pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS product identification against standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlated with high pregomisin accumulation in stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for dibenzylbutane precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with SchLAC14/15/16 in assay.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation step in dibenzylbutane lignan conversion toward pregomisin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: hydroxylated intermediates leading to schineolignin B and pregomisin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates dihydroguaiaretic acid on the aromatic ring positions C5 and C5′ to generate mono/polyhydroxylated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces hydroxyl groups required for downstream O-methylation and pregomisin formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchCYP719C7 was prioritized by correlation with pregomisin accumulation. 2. Microsomal assay in yeast showed conversion of dihydroguaiaretic acid to hydroxylated products 4 and 5. 3. Together with SchOMT02, it enables production of schineolignin B and pregomisin. 4. Evolutionary analysis showed SchCYP719C7 arose from lineage-specific duplication and functional specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; microsomal enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for pregomisin precursor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ancestral reconstruction suggested dual-function ancestors before specialization; grouped separately from SchCYP719G1b.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: O-methylation step producing schineolignin B and pregomisin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: hydroxylated products derived from dihydroguaiaretic acid by SchCYP719C7
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates hydroxylated lignan intermediates to form schineolignin B and pregomisin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces pregomisin, the direct precursor for octacyclic DBCOD scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchOMT02 was selected by strong coexpression with SchCYP719C7. 2. Purified recombinant SchOMT02 methylated SchCYP719C7-derived intermediates to generate schineolignin B and pregomisin. 3. This identifies SchOMT02 as the O-methylation enzyme leading to the direct substrate of SchCYP719G1b.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; purified protein assay; LC-MS product identification against standards
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with SchCYP719C7 and pregomisin-related tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruits and roots for DBCOD lignans; stems for pregomisin precursor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among five OMT candidates, only SchOMT02 was reported as functionally active in the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intramolecular aromatic C-C phenol coupling of pregomisin to form the octacyclic DBCOD scaffold of gomisin J
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts pregomisin to gomisin J and establishes the signature DBCOD lignan scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SchCYP719G1b was identified by strong correlation with gomisin J accumulation. 2. Yeast microsomes and transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana confirmed conversion of pregomisin to gomisin J. 3. The enzyme showed strict substrate selectivity, requiring para-hydroxyl groups in pregomisin. 4. QM/MM and mutagenesis analyses supported a diradical-mediated C2-C2′ coupling mechanism. 5. Evolutionary analyses indicated lineage-specific duplication and specialization of SchCYP719 genes, with SchCYP719G1b as a specialized C-C coupling enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/correlation analysis; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding; LC-MS/MS versus authentic standard; site-directed mutagenesis; QM/MM calculations
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with gomisin J accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate-feeding assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as the first plant-specialized metabolite enzyme known to form this octacyclic ring type; key residues Asp292 and Phe471 affect activity through substrate binding/localization.
